content.full_text:
State of California                                                    Franchise Tax Board

Memorandum

To    :  Robert Stern
        Fair Political Practices Commission
        1100 K Street
        Sacramento, CA  95814

From  :  Martin Huff

Subject:  Review of Audit Working Papers

                                                                        Date  :  July 22, 1976

                                                                        File No.:


     We need to establish basic guidelines and rules for the exchange of
information in order to insure the confidentiality of the information
exchanged.  To this end, a memorandum of understanding between our
departments is an essential prerequisite to the meeting requested in
your memo of July 16.

     Attached is a draft of a proposed agreement.

     We believe that, in addition to our providing information to the
Commission, the Commission has an obligation to keep us informed of
investigatory activity.  Incidents have already occurred where our
auditors walked into a situation either just after or during an FPPC
investigation.  We feel that it is essential that the staff of either
department be placed in the position of unknowingly acting in any way
to thwart or impede an audit or investigation already underway.
We also feel that duplication of effort should be avoided if at all
possible.

     We have also been asked for a meeting with the Attorney General's Office
on some of these same cases.  We would like to hold a joint meeting on
those cases if it meets with your approval.

     We will be ready to proceed with the meeting you requested as soon as
this agreement is finalized.


                    [signature]
                    Executive Officer

cc:  R. Summers


[illegible]

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION/FRANCHISE TAX BOARD

MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING

AGREEMENT


The Franchise Tax Board (FTB), in accordance with the Political Reform Act of

1974, Section 90005, agrees to furnish the Fair Political Practices Commission

(FPPC) with information for use in connection with any court proceeding or any

lawful investigation of any agency.

The FPPC agrees to advise the FTB of the initiation of an investigation of

any entity subject to audit by the FTB.  FTB in turn agrees not to initiate

an audit of that entity without first advising the FPPC.  FPPC also agrees

to furnish, as a result of such investigation, any information that might be

either pertinent to, or duplicative of, an FTB audit.

The information mutually exchanged under this Agreement shall be used solely

for the purposes prescribed in Section 90005 of said Act.

The FTB and the FPPC, in recognition of their mutual responsibilities to

protect the confidentiality of information exchanged in accordance with

statutory provisions applicable to such information, agree:

     1.  That information exchanges shall be made in accordance with

          mutually accepted procedures for requesting and providing

          information; and

     2.  To hold confidential any personally identifiable information

          examined, furnished, and/or exchanged with regard to:

          (a) audit working papers, or (b) other information exchanged

          before and during an audit and/or investigation.


-2-

Within the purposes prescribed in Section 90005 of said Act, nothing in this

Agreement will prohibit the use of such information directly or indirectly

in court proceedings.

The FPPC and FTB mutually agree to provide each other written guidelines as

to the confidentiality and security of data provided under this Agreement.

The FTB and the FPPC recognize their mutual responsibilities to protect

the confidentiality of political reform audit working papers and other

information exchanged before and during an audit or investigation, as

provided by law, and to assure that such information is disclosed only to

those persons, and for such purposes, as are authorized by law. In recogni-

tion of these responsibilities, each party to this Agreement shall, when

requested by the other party, review with the other party its security

measures to protect the confidentiality of political reform audit and

investigation working papers and related information made available pursuant

to this Agreement.

The conditions of the Agreement are subject to the provisions of the Political

Reform Act of 1974. This Agreement may be terminated or modified at the

discretion of the Executive Officer of the Franchise Tax Board or of the

Executive Director of the Fair Political Practices Commission due to changes

in State statutes and regulations or whenever in the administration of this

law either party deems such action appropriate.


-3-

This Agreement shall become effective upon execution by the Executive
Officer of the Franchise Tax Board and the Executive Director of the Fair
Political Practices Commission.


APPROVED:


_________________________          _________________________

Executive Officer                 Executive Director
Franchise Tax Board               Fair Political Practices Commission

Signed at Sacramento, California  Signed at Sacramento, California

This 22 day of July, 1976        This ____ day of __________, 19__


[illegible]
